title: Follow-ups to within-tier model-failover: true-model provenance in the dossier, split exit-85 quota-vs-prompt, and llm-calls model_index
root_cause: go-review of the within-tier model-failover (feat/within-tier-model-fallback) confirmed the feature correct but flagged 3 residual gaps deferred from that PR: (1) PROVENANCE — when the model axis engages (e.g. fable walls → opus serves), core.PhaseResponse.ResolvedModel still records the PRE-dispatch tier resolution (runner.go:644-645,801 from tieredRes.Tier), and dispatchModelFailover's `used` return is discarded at engine.go's call site (`_, code := ...`); the true serving model survives only in llm-calls.ndjson + a transient stderr line, so phase-timing.json/dossier say 'fable/deep' when opus served — a silently-wrong provenance field against the ADR-0072 no-fabrication posture. (2) exit-85 is OVERLOADED — ExitUnknownPrompt=85 is raised for BOTH a genuine quota wall (driver_tmux_repl.go:694-698) AND the auto-responder's 'unknown interactive prompt, abandon run' escalation (driver_tmux_repl.go:647-649); dispatchModelFailover advances on both, but switching models won't help a prompt-shape problem — it burns up to len(chain)-1 wasted attempts. Mirrors the SAME ambiguity already present at the CLI/tier-fallback layer, but the new inner axis multiplies the blast radius. (3) llm-calls.ndjson Attempt field collides — recordTokenUsage writes req.Attempt (set once per Launch by the CLI/tier axis) for every model-chain iteration, so 3 model attempts within one Launch share the same attempt number, distinguished only by the model field; no current consumer keys on (agent,attempt) so latent, but S6/S7 rollups can't reconstruct the fallback shape.
evidence: go-review of feat/within-tier-model-fallback (2026-07-18): core/ports.go BridgeResponse (no serving-model field); internal/phases/runner/runner.go:644-645,801 (ResolvedModel from tieredRes.Tier, pre-dispatch); internal/bridge/engine.go call site discards dispatchModelFailover `used`; internal/bridge/model_failover.go:38 (advances on ExitUnknownPrompt); go/internal/bridge/exitcodes.go:32 + driver_tmux_repl.go:647-649,694-698 (85 dual-purpose); engine.go recordTokenUsage writes req.Attempt.
fix: (1) Add core.BridgeResponse.ResolvedModel (concrete serving model); engine sets it from dispatchModelFailover's `used`; runner prefers bres.ResolvedModel over the tier when non-empty for PhaseResponse.ResolvedModel + the dossier — CAREFUL: this changes ResolvedModel semantics from tier-name to concrete-model; audit consumers first. (2) Split the 85 space (or thread a reason code) so model-failover advances ONLY on the genuine-quota-wall variant, not the interactive-prompt escalation — coordinate with the CLI/tier-fallback layer which shares the ambiguity. (3) Add a model_index (or per-model sub-attempt) to the llm-calls.ndjson S3 record so a Launch's model-chain steps are distinguishable from CLI/tier retries.
acceptance: Dossier/phase-timing records the ACTUAL serving model when the model axis engaged (fable→opus → records opus), verified by a runner test; no consumer of the old tier-name semantics breaks (audit first).
A 85-from-interactive-prompt does NOT trigger model-failover (only the quota-wall variant does); regression test.
llm-calls.ndjson distinguishes model-chain steps from CLI/tier retries; test asserts the record shape.
go test -race touched pkgs + acs-durable green.
source: go-review of feat/within-tier-model-fallback 2026-07-18 (HIGH SessionName bug + empty-chain floor already fixed in that PR; these 3 MEDIUM/LOW deferred).
